name: Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail
name_original: Sant Antoni de Portmany — circular por Sant Rafel y Santa Gertrudis de Fruitera
description: Explore Ibiza's interior on the moderate Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail, a 23.6-mile mountain biking route with varied terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sant Antoni De Portmany offers a diverse landscape characterized by coastal trails, rolling hills, and Mediterranean pine forests. The region features varied terrain, from smoother paths suitable for leisure rides to more challenging ascents and descents. Riders can expect routes that navigate through rural areas, offering views of the coastline and inland scenery. The elevation changes are generally moderate, with some routes including more significant climbs. Best mountain bike trails around Sant Antoni De Portmany The most popular mountain bike trail is Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail, a 23.6 miles (38.0 km) route that takes about 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate trail explores the island's interior, passing through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Entrance/Exit Rafal Trobat Valley – Capelleta d'en Serra loop from Can Bagot, a difficult 52.8 miles (85.1 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through valleys and forested areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Scenic Forest Trails – Cala Salada loop from Sant Antoni de Portmany, a 25.1 miles (40.4 km) trail leading through coastal forests and offering sea views, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Mountain biking around Sant Antoni De Portmany is defined by coastal paths, inland hills, and Mediterranean woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Sant Antoni De Portmany are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Antoni De Portmany's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails around Sant Antoni De Portmany, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: Yes, Sant Antoni De Portmany offers 14 easy mountain bike trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them ideal for beginners or family outings. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed in the data, easy trails are typically suitable.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 12 difficult mountain bike trails. A notable challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31995873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance/Exit Rafal Trobat Valley – Capelleta d'en Serra loop from Can Bagot&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 85.1 km and features significant elevation gain through valleys and forested areas.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with good viewpoints in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: Many trails in the region offer scenic views, particularly those along the coast or through elevated areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19423164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Forest Trails – Cala Salada loop from Sant Antoni de Portmany&lt;/a&gt; provides sea views as it winds through coastal forests. Additionally, you might find panoramic vistas from highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3809407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Torretes Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Talaia&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible via some routes.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Sant Antoni De Portmany makes spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) ideal for mountain biking. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the weather is generally stable, providing comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring more rain.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Sant Antoni De Portmany are designed as loops. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009811570/santa-gertrudis-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 38 km loop exploring the island's interior. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38209201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capelleta d'en Serra – Sa Capelleta Forest Trail loop from Sant Antoni de Portmany&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails here?
A: The terrain around Sant Antoni De Portmany is diverse, featuring coastal paths, rolling hills, and Mediterranean pine forests. You'll encounter a mix of smoother paths and more technical sections with varied elevation changes, from moderate climbs to more significant ascents on difficult routes.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the coastline and forests, you can explore historical and natural landmarks. Some routes may pass near significant sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila)&lt;/a&gt; or offer views of various peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3809407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Torretes Peak&lt;/a&gt;. Always check the route details for specific points of interest.

Q: Where can I find mountain bike rentals in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: While specific rental shops are not listed in the guide data, Sant Antoni De Portmany is a popular tourist destination. You will find several bike rental services within the town that offer mountain bikes suitable for exploring the local trails. It's advisable to search online or inquire locally upon arrival.

Q: Are there less crowded mountain bike trails for a more peaceful ride?
A: While specific 'less crowded' trails are not identified, generally, routes further inland or those that are longer and more challenging tend to see fewer riders than popular coastal paths. Exploring some of the moderate to difficult routes, especially during off-peak hours or seasons, might offer a more tranquil experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sant Antoni De Portmany are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, from coastal paths with sea views to challenging inland climbs, and the well-maintained network of routes suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass near cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Many routes in the Sant Antoni De Portmany area, especially those that pass through or near villages and towns, will have opportunities to stop for refreshments. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009811570/santa-gertrudis-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; explores the island's interior, potentially passing through areas with local eateries. It's always a good idea to check your chosen route on a map for nearby amenities.

Q: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride in Sant Antoni De Portmany?
A: For mountain biking in Sant Antoni De Portmany, essential items include plenty of water, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), and a fully charged phone with GPS. Snacks are also recommended for longer rides, especially given the warm climate.
tips: This church was built at the end of the 18th century. built on a hill between Ibiza and Sant Antoni in a privileged location and offering beautiful views of Ibiza town.

Atypical church with a colorful façade and a large bell tower, built in the 18th century. The temple is located in the center of town, in the pedestrian area, and as a curious fact it should be noted that access to the choir is via an external staircase that starts from the porch and not from inside the temple.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail is designed to be completed in approximately 2 hours and 39 minutes on a mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. It's generally considered suitable for beginner to intermediate mountain bikers, offering a good balance of challenge and accessibility. While largely manageable, some sections may require dismounting and walking if you're not confident on unpaved or stony terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse landscape, including paved tracks, small paths, and sections through wooded areas and small towns. You'll encounter some tricky parts, such as downhill slopes, stony hills, and riverbeds, providing a varied cross-country biking experience.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail typically starts from San Antonio. While specific parking details aren't provided, San Antonio is a well-known town, so you should find public parking options there to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the most comfortable conditions for mountain biking in Ibiza, the best times of year are generally spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the island is less crowded than in peak summer.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a couple of significant religious buildings. You'll have the opportunity to see the Church of Sant Rafel de Sa Creu and the Church of Santa Gertrudis, offering glimpses into the local culture and architecture.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is outdoors, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular mountain biking route are not detailed. It's always best to check local signage or official Ibiza tourism guidelines regarding pets on trails to ensure compliance and a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the Santa Gertrudis Circular Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with and utilizes sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Santa Gertrudis de Fruitera and Camí Vell de Sant Rafel trails, among others.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and sections with tricky terrain like stony hills and riverbeds, this trail might be challenging for very young children or inexperienced family riders. It's more suited for families with older children who have some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a beautiful exploration of Ibiza's interior. You'll ride through typical island landscapes, charming wooded areas, and small towns, providing a unique perspective of the island's natural beauty away from the coast.

waypoints.name: Church of Sant Rafel de Sa Creu
